items.title: Colts Blue Zone Podcast 468: What's Next for Laiatu Latu and Tyler Warren?
items.summary: In this episode of the Colts Blue Zone Podcast, we break down news from OTAs and look ahead at the next steps for tight end Tyler Warren and edge rusher Laiatu Latu. Plus: two seismic NFL trades, the Colts sign a backup QB and we say goodbye to a Colts legend. The Colts Blue Zone [...]

items.title: Which Colts rookie are you most excited to see on the field?
items.summary: A new rookie class always holds some level of excitement. Conventional wisdom says at least several of these players won’t work out and some could even be cut before the season kicks off. Others will rise to the occasion and impress beyond expectations. The overall hope is that the Indianapolis Colts got things right. It […]

items.title: Colts expecting (needing) breakout third season from Laiatu Latu
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The bar isn’t very high, yet Laiatu Latu must clear it. With room to spare, truth be told. The Indianapolis Colts’ 2024 first-round draft pick – the first defensive player taken with the 15th overall selection – must be the tip of the spear for coordinator Lou Anarumo’s pass rush. And Latu knows [...]

items.title: Colts sign fourth-round picks Jalen Farmer, Bryce Boettcher
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts’ latest draft class is signed, sealed and delivered. The team signed its two remaining unsigned picks, both selected in the fourth round of the April draft. Guard Jalen Farmer signed a four-year deal worth a projected $5.591 million with a $1.21 million signing bonus, according to overthecap.com while linebacker Bryce [...]

items.title: Colts made QB roster moves on Monday, but it’s not what some may have anticipated
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts made roster moves at quarterback yesterday, except it didn’t involve 4th-year quarterback Anthony Richardson Sr., who remains on the trade block following his earlier offseason trade request. Rather, Indianapolis announced it signed backup quarterback Easton Stick, while waiving fellow quarterback Seth Henigan in a corresponding roster move. The Colts also signed free […]

items.title: Colts announce contracts for two remaining unsigned rookies of 2026 draft class
items.summary: The Indianapolis Colts announced that the team has reached an agreement with a pair of unsigned rookies: Kentucky offensive guard Jalen Farmer and Oregon linebacker Bryce Boettcher: This means that the club’s entire 8-man rookie draft class of 2026 has now been safely signed, during the team’s 2nd week of OTAs and before the team’s […]

items.title: Colts’ QB Daniel Jones takes another step forward in Achilles recovery during OTAs

items.title: Colts’ Gardner adds his two cents on the blockbuster NFL trade

Jamming the receiver early and throwing them out of their rhythm are keys to locking down a receiver. Yes, they are […]

items.title: Another week, more progress for Colts’ Daniel Jones
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – Daniel Jones’ rehab continued to progress without a hiccup. The Indianapolis Colts’ starting quarterback saw his first action in 7-on-7 work during Monday’s organized team activities (OTAs) practice. That was the next anticipated step as Jones recovers from Dec. 9 surgery to repair a torn right Achilles tendon. Prior to Monday, Jones’ participation [...]

items.summary: Berry spent all 13 years of his NFL career with the Baltimore Colts and was inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame in 1973.

items.title: Colts legend, Hall of Famer Raymond Berry dies at 93
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Colts and NFL have lost a legend and a gentleman. Raymond Berry, a trail-blazing receiver with the Baltimore Colts, has died. He was 93. Berry’s family made the announcement Monday in a message to the Pro Football Hall of Fame. Berry was a two-time world champion with the Colts and teamed with [...]

items.title: Colts' 'Kicking The Stigma' marks 1 year since Jim Irsay's death
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS -- It was on May 21, 2025, that Indianapolis Colts Owner Jim Irsay died in Southern California. Irsay had always been public about his struggle to stay clean and had donated $32 million since 2020 to “Kicking the Stigma," a campaign that acknowledges the challenges of mental health wellness and funds treatment and research. items.title: Colts headed to London in NFL's international slate
items.summary: INDIANAPOLIS – The Indianapolis Colts are headed overseas. Again.
